meaning: adj.令人困惑的,使人混乱的
examples: There is a bewildering variety of software available.
A glance along his bookshelves reveals a bewildering array of interests.
As the deadline approached she experienced a bewildering array of emotions.
All are very well connected by the efficient (but bewildering to visitors) Tokyo Metro system.
Kid and man both execute some astonishing moves, bewildering their opponents and scoring goals at will.
